name: bmad-bmb-workflow-builder
description: HITL evaluation suite for the Workflow & Skill Builder. Tests the builder's ability to create simple utilities, simple workflows, and complex workflows through conversational discovery.

evals.name: Build Simple JSON Validator Utility
evals.description: Tests the builder's ability to create a simple utility skill from a clear description. Validates classification as Simple Utility and correct structure generation.
evals.persona: Alex, 32, backend developer. Concise communicator, knows what they want, gives clear requirements. Prefers minimal interaction — just build it.
evals.goal: Create a simple utility skill that validates JSON files against a schema
evals.initial_input: I need a simple utility skill that validates JSON files against a provided schema. It should accept a file path and schema path, then return validation results as structured JSON.
success_criteria: Builder correctly classifies this as a Simple Utility
Builder asks about module context (standalone vs module)
Builder generates SKILL.md with Input/Output sections
Generated structure has scripts/ directory for validation logic
Builder offers to design HITL evals after build
failure_modes: Builder classifies as Simple Workflow or Complex Workflow
Builder creates unnecessary prompts/ directory
Builder skips classification phase
Builder doesn't ask about testing preference

evals.name: Build Complex Multi-Stage Workflow
evals.description: Tests the builder's ability to create a complex workflow with progressive disclosure, numbered stages, and config integration. This is the most comprehensive test.
evals.persona: Jordan, 40, technical architect. Thinks in systems, asks clarifying questions, wants to understand trade-offs. Gives detailed requirements but expects the builder to add structure.
evals.goal: Create a complex workflow for automated code review with multiple stages
evals.initial_input: I want to build a multi-stage code review workflow. It should discover what to review, plan the review strategy, execute the review with multiple passes, and generate a consolidated report. It should be part of a module called 'Code Quality' with code 'cq'.
success_criteria: Builder correctly classifies as Complex Workflow
Builder identifies 4+ stages from the description
Builder asks about headless mode support
Builder generates routing SKILL.md with stages table
Builder creates numbered stage prompts in prompts/
Builder generates manifest.yaml with module code 'cq'
Config integration includes bmad-init loading
Builder offers quality scan after build
failure_modes: Builder puts all stages inline in SKILL.md
Builder forgets manifest.yaml for module-based workflow
Builder skips asking about headless mode
Builder doesn't number stage prompts

evals.name: Build Simple PR Creation Workflow
evals.description: Tests the builder's ability to create a simple workflow with inline steps. Should correctly identify this doesn't need progressive disclosure.
evals.persona: Sam, 26, junior developer. Friendly, asks questions, needs some guidance on what's needed. Communicates casually.
evals.goal: Create a simple workflow for creating standardized pull requests
evals.initial_input: Hey! I want a skill that helps create pull requests with a standard template. It should check the branch, gather info, and create the PR with gh cli.
success_criteria: Builder correctly classifies as Simple Workflow
Builder creates SKILL.md with numbered inline steps
Builder asks about module context
Builder doesn't create prompts/ directory (unnecessary for simple workflow)
Generated workflow references gh CLI appropriately
failure_modes: Builder over-engineers as Complex Workflow
Builder creates separate prompt files for each step
Builder skips gathering requirements about the PR template

evals.name: Convert Existing Non-BMad Skill
evals.description: Tests the builder's ability to accept an existing non-BMad skill as input and convert it to BMad-compliant structure.
evals.persona: Morgan, 35, DevOps engineer. Direct, experienced with automation tools, skeptical but open to new approaches. Provides existing code as input.
evals.goal: Convert an existing deployment script into a BMad-compliant workflow
evals.initial_input: I have an existing deployment script I want to convert into a BMad skill. Here's what it does: it checks environment variables, runs database migrations, deploys the app, and runs smoke tests. Can you convert this into a proper BMad workflow?
success_criteria: Builder recognizes this as a conversion task
Builder asks to see the existing script or accepts the description
Builder correctly classifies the skill type
Builder identifies what needs to be adapted for BMad compliance
Generated output includes proper config integration
failure_modes: Builder ignores the existing script context
Builder starts from scratch without analyzing the input
Builder doesn't ensure BMad standard conformance

evals.name: Quality Scan Existing Workflow
evals.description: Tests the quality optimizer routing — user wants to review/validate an existing workflow, not build a new one.
evals.persona: Riley, 38, QA engineer. Methodical, detail-oriented, wants comprehensive validation. Expects structured output.
evals.goal: Run a quality scan on an existing workflow and get a report
evals.initial_input: I want to quality check the workflow at .claude/skills/bmad-bmb-workflow-builder/. Run a full scan and give me the report.
success_criteria: Builder routes to Quality Optimizer, not Build Process
Builder loads quality-optimizer.md prompt
Builder creates output directory for scan results
Builder spawns scanner subagents (doesn't read files directly)
Builder presents findings summary after scan completes
failure_modes: Builder tries to build a new workflow instead of scanning
Builder reads target files directly instead of delegating to scanners
Builder skips pre-scan checks (uncommitted changes, functioning verification)

evals.name: Headless/Autonomous Quality Scan
evals.description: Tests headless mode detection and autonomous quality scan execution.
evals.persona: System automation — no persona, this is a headless invocation.
evals.goal: Run autonomous quality scan and get structured JSON output
success_criteria: Builder detects autonomous mode from --autonomous flag
Builder skips all interactive questions
Builder outputs structured JSON summary
Builder does NOT offer remediation or next steps
Output includes report_file path
failure_modes: Builder asks questions in autonomous mode
Builder offers remediation in autonomous mode
Builder outputs conversational text instead of structured JSON

evals.name: Handle Vague Input Through Discovery
evals.description: Tests the builder's ability to handle vague initial input and guide the user through discovery to classify and build.
evals.persona: Casey, 29, product manager. Non-technical, describes things in business terms, needs guidance on technical decisions. Enthusiastic but vague.
evals.goal: Build a skill from a vague description through guided discovery
evals.initial_input: I want something that helps our team do better code reviews. Like, make them more consistent and thorough.
success_criteria: Builder asks clarifying questions about scope and requirements
Builder guides user through skill type classification
Builder explains classification decision with reasoning
Builder gathers enough requirements to draft an outline
Builder presents draft and asks for refinement before building
failure_modes: Builder immediately starts building without discovery
Builder classifies without enough information
Builder skips the draft/refine phase
Builder uses technical jargon without explaining
